mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the risk-free discount rate applied to equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.05) relative to baseline during rate cuts, or Rank IC falls below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points within a single month signals abrupt labor market deterioration, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns as earnings expectations collapse.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns remain positive in > 55% of instances where the unemployment rate crosses the 0.5pp monthly threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand recovery, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter through sustained corporate revenue growth.
failure_condition: The joint positive signal fails to produce positive 60-day returns in > 60% of occurrences, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the index of industrial production during a late-cycle expansion phase indicates supply-side bottlenecks and impending recession, causing immediate negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in > 50% of late-cycle industrial production contractions, or the signal shows zero predictive power outside the specified regime.

mechanism_narrative: A falling federal funds rate combined with stable or declining consumer price inflation confirms real rate compression, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month as equity risk premiums narrow.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields negative 20-day returns in > 55% of cases, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent month-over-month declines in total nonfarm payrolls directly reduce aggregate consumption capacity, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter as corporate earnings forecasts are downgraded.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns show no negative correlation with payroll declines, or the Rank IC remains below 0.01 across all rolling 3-year evaluation windows.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly real GDP growth rate exceeding 3.0 percent signals robust economic momentum, triggering immediate positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week as market participants price in higher near-term earnings.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in > 60% of quarters where GDP growth surpasses the 3.0 percent threshold, or the signal fails to beat a buy-and-hold benchmark.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production alongside expanding retail sales during a post-recession recovery phase confirms synchronized supply and demand expansion, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The synchronized expansion signal produces negative 20-day returns in > 50% of post-recession recovery periods, or the predictive alpha decays to zero within 10 trading days.
